mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp, invalidating the risk-off signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either RSAFS or HOUST declines, or when the interaction term shows negative predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.0% reflect sustained economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% correlates with negative or flat 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, breaking the growth-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond 3.5% annually, central banks tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.5%, negating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction and rising input costs, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or positive returns occur in over 60% of such instances.
